project: TESCAN 3DIM - New Trends in Image and 3D Data Analysis
title: TESCAN 3DIM - Nove trendy v analyze obrazovych a 3D dat

english_title.label: English title
english_title.text: TESCAN 3DIM - New Trends in Image and 3D Data Analysis

keywords.label: Keywords
keywords.text: deep learning, neural networks, image analysis, polygonal mesh, 3D model, geometry processing, shape models

abstract.label: Abstract
abstract.text: This contractual research aims to analyze new trends in deep learning for recognition and automated analysis of image and 3D geometric data with a focus on various types of 3D shape representations and large image data whose processing is demanding; furthermore, in the experimental verification of the suitability of using selected new methods for the needs and tasks defined by the Partner and consultations of the Partner's existing solutions and technical assistance in their innovation.

team_members.label: Team members
team_members.text: Spanel Michal, doc. Ing., Ph.D. (DCGM) – research leader Kubik Tibor, Ing. (DCGM) Pukanec David, Ing. (DCGM) Silling Petr, Ing. (DCGM)

publication_results.text: - SILLING, P.; SPANEL, M. DEMIS: Electron Microscopy Image Stitching using Deep Learning Features and Global Optimisation. Proceedings of the 18th International Joint Conference on Biomedical Engineering Systems and Technologies - BIOIMAGING. Porto: Institute for Systems and Technologies of Information, Control and Communication, 2025. p. 255-256. ISBN: 978-989-758-731-3. Detail
